title: 5 of the Most Romantic Destinations for the Newlywed on Their Honeymoon
excerpt: Discover the 5 most romantic destinations for newlyweds in 2025—from Santorini's caldera sunsets to Maldives overwater bungalows. Your ultimate honeymoon guide.
content:
5 of the Most Romantic Destinations for the Newlywed on Their Honeymoon

Why Choosing the Right Honeymoon Destination Changes Everything

A honeymoon is not simply a holiday. It is the first chapter of a shared life, and the setting you choose will frame every memory you carry forward together. The most romantic destinations for newlyweds share certain irreducible qualities: seclusion or the illusion of it, sensory beauty that demands you be present, world-class hospitality, and an atmosphere that makes ordinary moments feel extraordinary.

With so many destinations vying for honeymoon budgets in 2025, narrowing the field requires honest criteria. This guide applies three: verifiable romantic experiences backed by real pricing, optimal timing to avoid the crowds and heat that flatten a trip, and the practical logistics—flights, transfers, accommodation tiers—that determine whether a dream holiday actually runs smoothly.

Whether you are drawn to the volcanic drama of the Greek islands, the utter isolation of a Maldivian atoll, the cultured elegance of Paris, the spiritual lushness of Bali, or a destination that surprises you entirely, the five choices below represent the global gold standard for newlywed travel in 2025 and 2026. Each one has been assessed for weather windows, romance-to-value ratio, and the logistical realities that travel brochures prefer to omit.

Before you book, consider pairing your destination research with a reliable travel planning service that handles airport transfers and private ground transportation—a detail that quietly determines how relaxed you feel from the moment you land. For curated luxury ground transport options, explore our private car service recommendations to ensure your arrival matches the standard of the destination itself.

Santorini, Greece — The Caldera Classic

No destination on earth has been more consistently voted the world's most romantic than Santorini. That consensus is not hyperbole—it is earned by a specific collision of geography and architecture that exists nowhere else. The island sits atop a partially submerged volcanic caldera, and the iconic white-washed villages of Oia and Imerovigli cling to its rim 300 metres above a sea that shifts between cobalt, turquoise, and deep navy depending on the hour.

The famous Oia sunset is genuinely spectacular, but arriving 90 minutes early for a position at the Kastro ruins is non-negotiable during peak season. A more intimate alternative: book dinner at Ammoudi Fish Tavern, 214 steps below Oia at the water's edge, where seafood for two runs €80–€120 and the sunset unfolds across the water rather than over a crowd. For daylight romance, a private catamaran charter on the caldera costs €350–€600 for a half day and typically includes a stop at the volcanic hot springs near Nea Kameni—an experience impossible to replicate on land.

The best time to visit is late April through June, or September through October. September is particularly compelling: sea temperatures hold at 25°C, tourist numbers drop by roughly 30% compared to August, and nightly rates at caldera properties fall meaningfully. July and August push temperatures above 35°C and see cruise ship arrivals approach the 8,000-passenger daily cap introduced in 2024—crowds that visibly congest the narrow lanes of Oia.

Accommodation ranges from €80 per night for guesthouses in Fira to €1,200 or more for cliff-edge cave suites at properties like Canaves Oia Suites (from €700/night with private plunge pools) and Andronis Luxury Suites (from €800/night). Mid-tier options in Firostefani and Imerovigli offer caldera views for €200–€400 per night—exceptional value for the setting.

Getting there is straightforward: Aegean Airlines and Sky Express operate 45-minute flights from Athens for €40–€120 one-way, or SeaJets runs a high-speed ferry from Piraeus in approximately 4.5 hours at €75–€100 per person. On the island, an ATV rental (€30–€50/day) suits the terrain well, though narrow roads and tourist traffic make early-morning and evening drives far more pleasurable than midday. Don't overlook the Santo Wines Winery at Pyrgos, where tasting flights (€18–€45/person) come with panoramic caldera views that rival anything in Oia—and far fewer selfie sticks.

The Maldives — Overwater Seclusion Perfected

There is a reason the Maldives has become synonymous with honeymoon travel: the architecture of a private overwater bungalow, suspended above a lagoon of such clarity that fish are visible through glass floor panels from your bed, creates a physical environment designed almost entirely around intimacy. The country consists of 1,192 coral islands spread across 26 atolls in the Indian Ocean, and the best resorts operate on islands so small that solitude is structural rather than optional.

The dry season runs from November through April, with February through April offering the ideal combination of guaranteed sunshine, water temperatures holding at 28–30°C, and pricing that eases slightly from the December–January peak. Visibility for snorkelling and diving reaches 30–50 metres across most house reefs during this window. Budget-conscious honeymooners should note that the wet season (May–October) brings 25–40% reductions in resort rates and typically only afternoon showers of one to two hours—leaving mornings reliably clear.

The resort tier you choose shapes the entire experience. Mid-range properties with overwater bungalows start around $400–$800 per night; luxury benchmarks include Gili Lankanfushi (from $1,500/night with a personal butler), Soneva Jani in Noonu Atoll (from $2,500/night, with a retractable roof for stargazing from bed), and Conrad Maldives Rangali Island (from $1,200/night), which is home to the Ithaa Undersea Restaurant—a glass-tunnel dining room 5 metres below the lagoon surface where dinner starts from $400 per person and reservations routinely fill three to six months ahead. Budget travellers can stay on local guesthouses islands such as Maafushi for $80–$200 per night, though alcohol is only available at resort islands by Maldivian law.

Logistics require attention. Most visitors enter through Velana International Airport in Malé. Transfer to your resort is either by speedboat ($100–$300 per person round trip) or by seaplane for properties in the outer northern and southern atolls ($400–$600 per person round trip)—seaplane operations are daylight-only, meaning your flight arrival time determines whether a same-day transfer is even possible. Flights from the UK cost £700–£1,800 return; from the US, expect $900–$2,500 with connections typically routing through Doha, Dubai, or Colombo.

For a seven-night luxury honeymoon, budget realistically: flights from Europe €1,400–€3,600, accommodation €7,000–€21,000, and activities plus dining €1,000–€3,000 if not on an all-inclusive package. Most five-star properties bundle meals, making the total more manageable. No visa is required on arrival for citizens of the US, EU, UK, Australia, and most other nationalities—a 30-day stamp is issued free of charge upon landing.

Paris, France — Romance as a Way of Life

Paris earns its reputation not through a single iconic moment but through the accumulated weight of an entire culture that takes beauty, food, and intimacy seriously. Haussmann's broad boulevards, the Seine's gentle bends, the weight of institutions like the Louvre—home to the Mona Lisa and Venus de Milo—and the neighbourhood cadence of Saint-Germain-des-Prés all conspire to make ordinary afternoons feel cinematic.

April through June is the most romantic window: cherry blossoms persist into May, the Luxembourg Gardens come into full bloom, temperatures settle between 15–22°C, and the terrasse dining culture is in full swing. September and October offer a golden-lit alternative with lighter crowds than summer and harvest menus appearing in bistro windows. July and August increasingly bring dangerous heat waves—2023 and 2024 both saw temperatures exceed 35°C—and many neighbourhood restaurants close entirely in August as Parisians leave the city.

The Eiffel Tower remains the centrepiece of any romantic itinerary. Summit entry via lift costs €35.30 per person in 2025; timed entry must be booked two to three months in advance for peak season visits. Each evening after dark, the tower sparkles for five minutes every hour—visible for free from the Trocadéro esplanade, where the crowd atmosphere is actually part of the magic. For a more structured evening, the one-Michelin-star Le Jules Verne restaurant occupies the tower's second floor; dinner runs €230–€350 per person and requires weeks of advance booking.

A Seine River dinner cruise aboard Bateaux Parisiens (€110–€195 per person with live music) covers the city's most illuminated stretches and provides an effortless overview of monuments that would take three days to cover on foot. Day trips to the Palace of Versailles are straightforward via RER C (€4.10 each way); palace entry is €21 per person, and the gardens are free on most weekdays.

Accommodation in Paris rewards research. The 6th arrondissement (Saint-Germain), 1st (Marais and Louvre), and 7th (Eiffel Tower) are the most romantic bases. Boutique options include Hôtel Particulier Montmartre (from €350/night, a hidden mansion feel) and La Réserve Paris (from €700/night, 40 rooms of genuine intimacy). For palace-level luxury, Le Meurice and Hôtel Plaza Athénée both begin around €800–€900 per night.

Practical movement around the city is simple: a Navigo Découverte weekly pass costs €30 per person in 2025 and covers unlimited Metro, RER, and bus travel across zones 1–5, including Versailles. From Charles de Gaulle Airport, a fixed-rate taxi to central Paris costs €50–€70, while the RER B train takes 35 minutes for €11.80 per person. For couples who prefer door-to-door comfort from the airport—particularly with luggage and the disorientation of arrival—a pre-booked private transfer eliminates the stress of navigating the Metro on your first day. Our guide to private airport transfers covers the best options for seamless arrivals into major European cities.

Bali, Indonesia — Spiritual Beauty at Extraordinary Value

Bali is the rare destination that delivers five-star romantic experiences at price points accessible to honeymooners on almost any budget. The island's appeal is genuinely multi-layered: there is the Hindu spirituality embedded in temple ceremonies and offerings that appear on doorsteps each morning, the green theatrical beauty of Ubud's terraced rice paddies, the dramatic clifftop hotels of Uluwatu perched over Indian Ocean surf breaks, and a spa culture so deeply embedded in the local economy that a world-class couples massage for two can cost under €50.

The best timing for a honeymoon is May–June or September, when the dry season produces reliable sunshine, prices sit 20–30% below the peak July–August school holiday window, and the crowds at key sites are manageable. The Tegallalang Rice Terrace near Ubud is most magical at sunrise—entry is IDR 50,000 (approximately $3) per person since 2025's new fee introduction—and the Uluwatu Kecak Fire Dance at sunset (IDR 150,000, approximately $9.50) remains one of the most theatrically stunning free-standing performances in Southeast Asia. At Tanah Lot, where a centuries-old sea temple sits on a rock stack surrounded by crashing waves, entry is IDR 75,000 (approximately $4.70) and the sunset views are genuinely world-class.

Bali's five main regions serve very different honeymoon personalities. Ubud suits couples drawn to culture, yoga, jungle walks, and the internal quiet of rice field mornings. Seminyak and Canggu offer beach club sunsets, sophisticated dining, and a polished urban-tropical atmosphere. Uluwatu delivers the most dramatic scenery on the island—cliff-edge infinity pools above open ocean—while Nusa Dua provides a calm, manicured resort enclave ideal for couples who want a pure beach retreat. Nusa Penida, accessible by fast boat in roughly 45 minutes from Sanur, adds a day trip dimension: Kelingking Beach's T-Rex-shaped cliffs are among the most photographed natural features in all of Indonesia, and manta ray snorkelling in the surrounding waters is reliably excellent.

Accommodation value in Bali is exceptional. A genuinely luxurious private villa with its own pool and daily breakfast in Seminyak or Ubud can be secured for €150–€350 per night—figures that would correspond to standard mid-range hotel rooms in Paris or Santorini. The upper tier of Bali luxury, represented by properties in Uluwatu, begins around €400–€600 per night for cliff-edge suites with unobstructed ocean views.

Flights from Europe run €600–€1,200 return via Ngurah Rai International Airport in Denpasar, typically connecting through Singapore, Kuala Lumpur, or Doha. Australians enjoy some of the most affordable access of any major destination—under AUD 500 return from Melbourne or Sydney during sales periods. On-arrival visas are available for most nationalities at $35 USD (2025 rate), granting 30 days with the option to extend. Ground transport between regions is best handled by hiring a local driver for the day (IDR 500,000–600,000, approximately $30–$40)—a far more romantic and practical option than navigating Bali's scooter-dense roads independently.

How to Plan the Perfect Honeymoon: Timing, Budget, and Booking Strategy

The difference between a honeymoon that exceeds expectations and one that generates polite conversation at dinner parties is almost always logistical. The destination matters, but the execution determines the experience.

Book accommodation first, everything else second. For Santorini caldera suites, Maldives overwater villas, and Paris Michelin-star restaurants, availability disappears six to twelve months ahead of peak season dates. If your wedding date is fixed, your honeymoon accommodation booking should follow within days of confirming the venue. For the Maldives specifically, seaplane transfer availability is as constrained as the resort rooms themselves—the two must be confirmed in tandem.

Travel in shoulder season wherever possible. For every destination on this list, the month immediately before or after peak season offers a combination of near-identical weather, meaningfully lower prices, and the absence of the kind of crowds that make a private romantic experience feel like a queue. September in Santorini, February in the Maldives, October in Paris, and May in Bali all represent objectively better honeymoon windows than the dates most couples default to.

Budget with full transparency. A common planning error is budgeting the accommodation and flights without accounting for the experience layer: a private catamaran in Santorini (€350–€600), a sandbank dinner in the Maldives (€200–€600), a Seine dinner cruise in Paris (€110–€195 per person), or a Mount Batur sunrise trek in Bali ($50–$75 per person) are not optional luxuries on a honeymoon—they are the reason you chose the destination. Build them into the budget from the start.

Ground transportation is the hidden quality variable. The transition from airport to hotel, or between destinations within a country, is where budget decisions manifest most visibly. Arriving in Paris after an overnight transatlantic flight to find yourselves managing luggage on the RER B sets a specific tone. A pre-arranged private transfer sets a different one. For couples investing in a once-in-a-lifetime trip, coordinating arrival logistics through a dedicated luxury transport service—particularly for multi-destination itineraries—is worth factoring into the overall budget. Explore our curated recommendations for private chauffeur services and premium airport transfer options to complete your honeymoon planning from departure to destination.

Travel insurance is non-negotiable. Wedding postponements, flight cancellations, and medical incidents happen. A comprehensive policy covering cancellation, medical evacuation (critical for the Maldives outer atolls), and trip interruption should be purchased at the time of booking, not as an afterthought. For the Maldives and Bali particularly, medical evacuation coverage with a minimum of $500,000 is the industry-recommended minimum.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the most romantic honeymoon destination in the world?

Santorini, Greece and the Maldives consistently rank as the world's top two honeymoon destinations in global travel surveys. Santorini offers unmatched scenic drama with its caldera views, volcanic beaches, and iconic sunsets from Oia. The Maldives delivers unparalleled seclusion via private overwater bungalows above crystal-clear Indian Ocean lagoons. The right choice depends on your priorities: Santorini suits couples who want culture, history, and great food alongside the romance; the Maldives is for those who want total isolation and ocean immersion.

When is the best time to visit Santorini for a honeymoon?

Late April through June and September through October are the optimal windows. September is the single best month: sea temperatures reach 25°C, tourist numbers are roughly 30% lower than August, and caldera villa rates drop significantly. Avoid July and August when temperatures exceed 35°C, cruise passenger arrivals hit the 8,000-per-day cap, and prices surge 40–60% above shoulder-season levels.

How much does a honeymoon in the Maldives cost?

A seven-night luxury Maldives honeymoon for two typically costs €10,000–€30,000 inclusive of flights and accommodation. Flights from Europe run €1,400–€3,600 per couple return; luxury overwater villas range from $800 to $5,000+ per night depending on the resort. Budget-conscious couples can stay in local island guesthouses (such as Maafushi) for $80–$200 per night, though alcohol is only available at resort islands. February through April offers the best combination of dry-season weather and slightly reduced pricing versus the December–January peak.

Is Paris still one of the most romantic destinations for newlyweds?

Absolutely. Paris remains among the most romantic destinations globally in 2025. Its combination of world-class cuisine (including 10 restaurants with three Michelin stars), architectural beauty, Seine River ambiance, and a cultural attitude that treats public expressions of affection as normal makes it uniquely suited to honeymooners. April through June and September through October are the best periods. The city's compact size means you can cover extraordinary ground on foot or by Metro, and even mid-range experiences—a dinner in Le Marais, an evening at the Eiffel Tower—feel genuinely special.

Why is Bali considered a top romantic destination for newlyweds?

Bali offers a combination of spiritual atmosphere, extraordinary natural beauty, and exceptional value that few destinations can match. Private pool villas with daily breakfast cost €150–€350 per night—price points that correspond to standard rooms in Paris or Santorini. The island's diverse regions mean couples can combine a cultural base in Ubud with dramatic clifftop romance in Uluwatu and beach-club evenings in Seminyak within a single trip. Experiences such as a Kecak Fire Dance at sunset (approximately $9.50 entry) or a Mount Batur sunrise trek ($50–$75 per person) create memorable moments at a fraction of the cost of comparable experiences in European destinations.

Do I need a visa for a Maldives or Bali honeymoon?

Neither destination requires advance visa applications for most nationalities. The Maldives issues a free 30-day on-arrival visa to citizens of the US, EU, UK, Australia, and most other countries—you simply need a valid onward ticket and proof of accommodation. Bali (Indonesia) offers a Visa on Arrival for $35 USD at Ngurah Rai International Airport, valid for 30 days and extendable for an additional 30 days at an immigration office. Both policies were current as of 2025 but should be confirmed via official embassy sources before travel.

How far in advance should honeymooners book their trip?

For peak-season travel (June–August for Santorini and Paris; December–January for the Maldives), book accommodation six to twelve months in advance. Specific high-demand experiences require even more lead time: the Ithaa Undersea Restaurant in the Maldives books out three to six months ahead; Le Jules Verne at the Eiffel Tower requires weeks of advance reservation; and Santorini's cliff-edge cave suites at properties like Canaves Oia or Andronis fill quickly once peak-season dates open. Shoulder-season trips can be organised with three to four months' notice, but the principle holds: accommodation first, flights second, experiences third.
